intervention_families.description: Convert wrapper_key_drift and envelope_omission rows to strict canonical tool_calls envelope while preserving prompt diversity.
intervention_families.safety_notes: High-structure, low-policing repair path. Keep diverse phrasing and avoid imperative global constraints.

intervention_families.description: Train conversions from answer-content substitution into canonical call payloads for same prompt intent/tool args.
intervention_families.safety_notes: Most leverage; requires careful style-preservation and family balancing to avoid local coercion.

intervention_families.description: Teach fallback behavior: when unsure of result, still emit canonical tool_calls for acquisition step instead of direct answer.
intervention_families.safety_notes: Potentially robust if implemented with varied, non-imperative exemplars and no blacklist policing.

intervention_families.description: Expand canonical activation beyond brittle literal anchor (e.g., tool_calls token trigger) through paraphrastic canonical targets.
intervention_families.safety_notes: Useful but risky; can drift into hidden template homogenization if not tightly bounded.

intervention_families.description: Global directives/blacklists forcing only canonical structure and forbidding alternatives.
intervention_families.safety_notes: High probability of i4/i5-style collapse dynamics; should remain disallowed.

early_collapse_signals: rapid rise in payload_not_parsed/invalid_json with simultaneous drop in exact_valid over first bounded eval slice
collapse of behavior diversity: top-1 behavioral category share > 0.70 in targeted tools
sharp increase in scalar/direct-answer substitutions while near-canonical repairs do not rise
drop in no-call correctness below established invariant or any wrapper leakage > 0
new dominance of single lexical anchor family with loss of paraphrastic canonical success
